stories.title: Scientists showed horses silent videos of wolves and found their hearts raced even when...
stories.summary: Horses exhibit an intriguing phenomenon when identifying predators on video screens—they experience heightened heart rates indicative of internal stress while showing no outward signs of fear. Notably, male horses and dominant herd members trigger stronger responses, challenging traditional views of prey animals' panic-driven behavior. Grasping this psychological disconnect is essential for improving horse welfare and ensuring rider safety.

stories.title: Quote of the day by Napoleon Bonaparte: 'The throne is only a piece of wood...'
stories.summary: Napoleon Bonaparte grasped that real power emerges from the collective imagination and faith of the people. He realized that symbols, like thrones, lack intrinsic authority. This principle of shared belief is fundamental to today’s economies and political structures. When public trust wanes, it leads to the depreciation of institutions and currencies. Ultimately, through the collective memory of society, Napoleon’s legend endured despite the fall of his empire.

stories.title: This ordinary pen helped save Apollo 11 astronauts, now it has sold at auction for an $857,600

It exemplifies the ingenuity and urgent problem-solving that marked space exploration history.

Escalating Iran-US conflict and rising oil prices added to investor caution. Foreign investors remained net sellers of Indian equities on Thursday. Analysts advise caution due to market range-bound construction and rupee weakness.

stories.summary: Gold prices edged higher on MCX while silver traded slightly lower on Friday. Renewed U.S.-Iran fighting lifted oil prices, stoking inflation concerns and rate hike expectations. MCX silver futures for September 2026 delivery were down Rs 745. Gold futures for August 2026 delivery declined by Rs 385. For the week, gold declined Rs 3,000 and silver Rs 7,000.

stories.title: Tech Mahindra shares jump 3% after Q1 earnings beat estimates. What Nomura, Nuvama, other brokerages now expect

stories.title: Jio Financial Services shares rally 6% after stellar Q1 results. Buy, sell or hold the stock?

Consolidated net profit jumped 155% year-on-year to Rs 830 crore, while revenue from operations soared 227% to Rs 2,004 crore. Interest income, fee and commission income, and profit before tax also recorded robust growth, reflecting strong business momentum across its lending and financial services businesses.

stories.title: Wipro shares fall over 3% after Q1 results. Why have Nomura, Motilal & others cut target prices?

Motilal Oswal maintained a Neutral rating, citing another challenging year ahead. While analysts flagged macro uncertainty and sluggish deal ramp-ups, they believe AI-led demand and attractive valuations could support the stock over the long term.

stories.title: Investors are watching Jio but Mukesh Ambani-led RIL’s Q1 surprise may come from refining
stories.summary: Reliance Industries' upcoming first-quarter earnings may depend on refining gains. Jio's steady expansion is expected to provide a stable growth engine for the company. Refining margins surged dramatically, raising expectations for the oil-to-chemicals business. However, planned refinery shutdowns could impact the full benefit of margin upswings. Investors will focus on how well RIL translates commodity margins into actual earnings.

stories.title: Deepfake scam rattles Sky Gold as subsidiary suffers Rs 11 crore loss after unauthorised fund transfer
stories.summary: Sky Gold shares fell after its subsidiary lost Rs 10.70 crore due to fraud. Unknown persons used deepfake techniques to impersonate a director and transfer funds. The subsidiary reported the incident to cybercrime authorities and is working with banks. This incident came to light on July 15, 2026, after verification. There is no indication of broader IT system breaches or data compromise.

stories.title: JGBs hold steady as investors weigh inflation, central bank signals

Investors are considering inflation risks and monetary policy signals from the Bank of Japan. Higher oil prices and steady economic data influenced global bond markets. stories.summary: The legislation, passed by a large majority in the upper house, permits unmarried male descendants from former imperial branch families to be adopted back into the imperial household. stories.title: Quote of the day by Arnold Schwarzenegger: "Just remember, you can't climb the ladder of success with…" - a powerful lesson on ambition, hard work and why success demands action
stories.summary: Arnold Schwarzenegger emphasized that success requires active effort, not just desire. His quote highlights the gap between ambition and actual work. Progress demands consistent action, which is more crucial than fleeting motivation. Breakthroughs often appear sudden but are built on years of preparation. This principle applies universally to any personal or professional aspiration.

stories.title: FBI arrests gangster Nitish Kaushal days after adding him to most wanted list

stories.title: Hang Seng crashes over 2% as tech selloff hits Asia markets. Why Hong Kong stocks are falling on Friday?
stories.summary: Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 2% by midday on Friday, weighed down by a global selloff in technology stocks as renewed inflation concerns dampened risk appetite. The Hang Seng Tech Index plunged 4%, with investors reducing exposure to technology and other growth stocks amid fears that higher inflation could lead to tighter monetary policy and higher interest rates.

stories.title: Global Market: South Korea's AI-driven stock frenzy fuels volatility, challenges market fundamentals
stories.summary: The sharp swings have unsettled investors and regulators alike, with market movements increasingly driven by leveraged investment products rather than corporate fundamentals. The volatility has also complicated the assessment of South Korean equities, particularly as the country remains a critical supplier of AI-related semiconductor technology.

stories.title: Global Market: Euro zone current account surplus widens in May; market impact likely limited
stories.summary: The euro zone's current account surplus increased in May, driven by stronger primary income. This rise offset a narrower trade surplus, according to European Central Bank data. On an unadjusted basis, a deficit was recorded for the month. Over twelve months, the surplus represented 1.7% of gross domestic product. Stock markets are not expected to react significantly to this data.

stories.title: JSW Steel Q1 Results: Net profit doubles to Rs 4,696 crore
stories.summary: JSW Steel has showcased impressive financial growth in the first quarter of the fiscal year, with its net profit skyrocketing to Rs 4,696 crore, a significant climb compared to previous figures. Total income surged to Rs 48,088 crore, a rise from Rs 43,497 crore last year. Additionally, crude steel production grew by three percent in the June quarter, as the company initiated a new steel plant project in Andhra Pradesh.

stories.title: Foreign inflows in Asian bonds surge to seven-month high in June
stories.summary: Asian bonds saw their largest foreign inflows in seven months during June. Lower oil prices and strong technology demand improved the region's economic outlook. Investors bought over eleven billion dollars in regional bonds, seeking safer fixed-income assets. South Korean and Indian bonds recorded substantial net foreign purchases. Indonesian bonds attracted significant cross-border inflows, driven by attractive yields.

stories.title: Zepto said to face sharply lower valuation for IPO
stories.summary: Zepto Ltd. seeks a lower IPO valuation, reflecting industry cash burn concerns. Foreign investors indicate interest at $4.5 billion, while domestic ones value it lower. This contrasts sharply with its $7 billion valuation from last October's fundraise. The company aims to raise $850 million through its initial public offering. Proceeds will fund network expansion and technological infrastructure improvements.

stories.title: After record $29 billion selloff, FIIs buying India again. Chris Wood explains why
stories.summary: After a record $29 billion foreign selloff, FIIs have turned net buyers of Indian equities in July. Jefferies' Christopher Wood says the earlier outflows were driven by the AI-led rally in Korea and Taiwan, adding that sustained inflows will depend on the AI trade cooling or Indian valuations becoming more attractive.

stories.title: Central Bank of India Q1 results: Profit rises 13% to Rs 1,324 cr
stories.summary: Central Bank of India announced a thirteen percent net profit increase for the June quarter. Total income rose to Rs 10,678 crore, and interest earned improved significantly. However, the bank's operating profit saw a decline from the previous year. Asset quality improved as gross non-performing assets moderated to two point six zero percent. The capital adequacy ratio also strengthened to eighteen point two eight percent during this period.

stories.title: Global Market: European shares fall as tech selloff, Middle East tensions weigh; Saab gains
stories.summary: European shares opened lower on Friday, heading for weekly losses. Escalating Middle East tensions and a tech stock selloff dampened investor sentiment. Technology stocks led the declines, dropping significantly across global markets. Investors shifted focus toward lagging sectors like luxury goods, which saw gains. Geopolitical tensions remained a key concern, impacting the global economic outlook.

stories.title: Global Market: ECB weighs inflation risks, digital Euro progress at July meeting

stories.title: Vedanta, demerged Vedanta Aluminium, Vedanta Oil & Gas get rating upgrades from CRISIL
stories.summary: CRISIL has upgraded ratings for Vedanta Aluminium Metal and Vedanta Oil and Gas. These upgrades reflect stronger business and financial risk profiles for the demerged entities. stories.title: In 1980, Mount St Helens buried a river valley in volcanic sediment. Then 58 beavers helped turn the wasteland into a thriving wetland
stories.summary: More than 40 years after the Mount St. Helens eruption buried a Washington river valley under volcanic sediment, 58 relocated beavers have helped restore the damaged landscape. Their dams and wetlands brought back native plants, fish and wildlife. However, conservationists fear a planned government project to raise a sediment retention structure could undo years of natural restoration.

A Spanish company built a bladeless wind turbine that sways instead of spinning, turning the vibrations caused by wind into electricity
stories.summary: A Spanish company has developed bladeless wind turbines that oscillate to generate electricity. These devices use vortex-induced vibrations, a phenomenon engineers usually avoid. The bladeless design offers a lighter, quieter, and mechanically simpler alternative to traditional turbines. While currently less powerful, they are suited for small-scale and remote energy needs. This innovative technology could complement existing renewable energy solutions in the future.

stories.title: As wars destroyed schools and libraries across Italy, one Roman official began copying ancient books and helped save centuries of knowledge from disappearing
stories.summary: War in sixth-century Italy destroyed knowledge, prompting Cassiodorus to act. He founded a monastery where monks copied and translated manuscripts. This work saved religious and secular writings from being lost forever. The Vivarium monastery became a vital center for preserving ancient texts. Cassiodorus's efforts ensured knowledge survived centuries of instability.

Not Philadelphia, this is where the United States started: Inside New Sweden, the long-lost colony that shaped America
stories.summary: Philadelphia's rich narrative isn't solely tied to its founding fathers' legacy; it also encompasses the short-lived Swedish colony of Nya Sverige. This settlement, which lasted seventeen years, pioneered the design of the log cabin, revolutionizing early American architecture. Furthermore, Swedish settlers introduced Lutheran Christianity and instigated critical early colonial rebellions, underscoring their significant yet brief impact in shaping America's foundations.

stories.title: He had the money to buy a private jet, yet this billionaire preferred economy flights, took home free packets of salt and pepper from restaurants

Kamprad reused tea bags and ate at IKEA cafeterias, avoiding expensive dining options. His thrifty habits stemmed from his upbringing in rural southern Sweden. This frugality also shaped IKEA's company culture and employee guidelines.

stories.title: Market wrap: Top gainers and losers on Nifty and Sensex today
stories.summary: Indian markets ended the week strongly as Sensex surged 964 points and Nifty gained 262 points, driven by robust Q1 earnings and IT buying. Analysts see improving momentum in large caps, with Nifty's breakout indicating potential upside towards 24,800 despite broader market weakness.

stories.title: Federal Bank among 4 stocks that hit 52-week highs and rallied up to 25% in a month
stories.summary: Sensex ended 96 points higher on Friday as four BSE 200 stocks—Oracle Financial Services, Adani Energy Solutions, BHEL and Federal Bank—hit fresh 52-week highs. The milestone reflects sustained buying interest, improving investor sentiment and continued bullish momentum in these counters.

stories.title: Shapoorji Pallonji Group completes long-delayed debt fundraising, sources say
stories.summary: The Shapoorji Pallonji Group has successfully wrapped up its fundraising campaign, garnering bids amounting to a remarkable 215 billion rupees for a three-year bond. This initiative is primarily designed to refinance the conglomerate's existing debts while investors have been reassured about the monetization of its stake in Tata Sons. Notably, a Mauritius-based entity played a crucial role in the rupee tranche investment.

stories.title: Rupee closes at 96.28 vs USD; logs sharpest weekly drop since May as oil price jump stings
stories.summary: The Indian rupee faced its most significant weekly drop since May, driven by rising oil prices and strong demand for the dollar. Increasing tensions between the U.S. and Iran exacerbated concerns over energy supplies, further straining the rupee's position. However, interventions from state-run banks provided some relief, temporarily halting its downward trend. Market participants are closely monitoring oil prices and the Reserve Bank of India’s approach to the rupee’s depreciation.

stories.title: Reliance Industries Q1 Results Live: RIL to announce earnings today; EBITDA may rise up to 10% on O2C, Jio growth
Rs 9,000 crore bet! Reliance Industries promoters increase stake by 0.5% in June quarter
stories.summary: Reliance Industries’ promoters increased their stake to 50.48% in the June quarter through market purchases worth an estimated Rs 8,500–9,000 crore. The move signals confidence despite the stock’s weak performance, earnings misses and challenges across retail and oil-to-chemicals businesses.

stories.title: Bitcoin falls nearly 2% to $63,000 as geopolitical tensions weigh on crypto markets
stories.summary: Ethereum fell 3.98% in the past 24 hours to trade at the $1,828 mark. Among the major altcoins, BNB, XRP, Solana, Tron, Hyperliquid, Dogecoin, and Cardano corrected up to 11.31%.
